title: DemiEngine Instruction Set Reference
description: Complete reference for all 63 implemented opcodes and instruction categories

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: DemiEngine currently implements **63 opcodes** across multiple categories. Instructions are written in hexadecimal format, with comments starting with `#`. The VM features 134 registers (R0-R7 basic, R0-R15 extended, plus special/system registers).

sections.title: Basic Syntax
sections.content:
- Instructions are hex-encoded bytes
- Comments start with `#`
- Registers: R0-R7 (basic), R0-R15 (extended)
- LOAD_IMM format: `opcode + register + immediate_value`
- Addresses are 8-bit (0-255)

sections.title: Basic Operations
sections.content:
```
00                    NOP  - No operation
FF                    HALT - Stop execution
01 <reg> <imm8>       LOAD_IMM - Load 8-bit immediate
04 <dst> <src>        MOV - Copy register value
```

sections.title: Arithmetic Operations
sections.content:
```
02 <dst> <src>        ADD - Add registers
03 <dst> <src>        SUB - Subtract registers
10 <dst> <src>        MUL - Multiply registers
11 <dst> <src>        DIV - Divide registers
12 <reg>              INC - Increment register
13 <reg>              DEC - Decrement register
```

sections.title: Memory Operations
sections.content:
```
06 <dst> <addr>       LOAD - Load from memory
07 <addr> <src>       STORE - Store to memory
20 <reg> <addr>       LEA - Load Effective Address
21 <reg> <addr>       SWAP - Swap register with memory
```

sections.title: Control Flow
sections.content:
```
05 <addr>             JMP - Unconditional jump
0A <reg1> <reg2>      CMP - Compare registers
0B <addr>             JZ - Jump if zero
0C <addr>             JNZ - Jump if not zero
0D <addr>             JS - Jump if sign
0E <addr>             JNS - Jump if not sign
0F <addr>             JC - Jump if carry
22 <addr>             JNC - Jump if no carry
23 <addr>             JO - Jump if overflow
24 <addr>             JNO - Jump if no overflow
25 <addr>             JG - Jump if greater
26 <addr>             JGE - Jump if greater/equal
27 <addr>             JL - Jump if less
28 <addr>             JLE - Jump if less/equal
```

sections.title: Stack Operations
sections.content:
```
08 <reg>              PUSH - Push register to stack
09 <reg>              POP - Pop from stack to register
1A <addr>             CALL - Call subroutine
1B                    RET - Return from subroutine
1C <reg>              PUSH_ARG - Push function argument
1D <reg>              POP_ARG - Pop function argument
1E <reg>              PUSH_FLAG - Push flags onto stack
1F <reg>              POP_FLAG - Pop flags from stack
```

sections.title: Bitwise Operations
sections.content:
```
14 <dst> <src>        AND - Bitwise AND
15 <dst> <src>        OR - Bitwise OR
16 <dst> <src>        XOR - Bitwise XOR
17 <reg>              NOT - Bitwise NOT
18 <reg> <amt>        SHL - Shift left
19 <reg> <amt>        SHR - Shift right
```

sections.title: I/O Operations
sections.content:
```
30 <dst> <port>       IN - Read from I/O port
31 <port> <src>       OUT - Write to I/O port
32 <dst> <port>       INB - Read byte from port
33 <port> <src>       OUTB - Write byte to port
34 <dst> <port>       INW - Read word from port
35 <port> <src>       OUTW - Write word to port
36 <dst> <port>       INL - Read long from port
37 <port> <src>       OUTL - Write long to port
38 <dst> <port>       INSTR - Read string from port
39 <port> <src>       OUTSTR - Write string to port
```

**Device Ports:**
- Port 1: Console (text I/O)
- Port 2: File device
- Port 3: Counter device
- Port 4: Serial port
- Ports 5-255: Available for custom devices

sections.title: FPU Operations (Floating-Point)
sections.content:
```
A0-A3    FLD, FST, FSTP        Load/Store FP values
A4-A6    FILD, FIST, FISTP     Integer conversion
A7-AA    FADD, FSUB, FMUL, FDIV Arithmetic
AB-AF    FSIN, FCOS, FTAN      Trigonometry
B0       FSQRT                 Square root
B1       FABS                  Absolute value
B2       FCHS                  Change sign
B3       FINIT                 Initialize FPU
B4-B6    FCLEX, FSTCW, FLDCW  Control/status
B7-B8    FCOMPP, FUCOMPP      Comparison
B9       FSTSW                 Store status word
```

**Note:** FPU uses a stack-based architecture with ST(0)-ST(7) registers.

sections.title: Register Usage Conventions
sections.content:
**General Purpose (R0-R7):**
- R0-R3: Function parameters and return values
- R4-R6: Local variables
- R7: Temporary or frame pointer

**Special Registers:**
- PC: Program counter (automatic)
- SP: Stack pointer (automatic)
- FP: Frame pointer (manual management)
- FLAGS: CPU flags (Zero, Carry, Sign, Overflow, etc.)

sections.title: Memory Layout
sections.content:
```
High Memory  ┌─────────┐
            │  Stack  │ ← SP (grows down)
            ├─────────┤
            │  Free   │
            ├─────────┤
            │  Heap   │
            ├─────────┤
            │  Data   │
            ├─────────┤
Low Memory  │  Code   │ ← PC starts here
            └─────────┘
```

sections.title: Common Programming Patterns
sections.content:
**Hello World:**
```hex
01 00 10              # LOAD_IMM R0, 0x10 (string addr)
39 01 00              # OUTSTR port=1, R0
FF                    # HALT
# String at 0x10:
48 65 6C 6C 6F 00     # "Hello\0"
```

**Loop Counter:**
```hex
01 00 0A              # LOAD_IMM R0, 10
01 01 00              # LOAD_IMM R1, 0
# Loop (0x06):
13 00                 # DEC R0
0A 00 01              # CMP R0, R1
0C 06                 # JNZ 0x06
FF                    # HALT
```

**Function Call:**
```hex
01 00 05              # LOAD_IMM R0, 5
1A 10                 # CALL 0x10
FF                    # HALT
# Function at 0x10:
12 00                 # INC R0
1B                    # RET
```

sections.title: Common Gotchas
sections.content:
1. **8-bit immediates**: LOAD_IMM only supports values 0-255
2. **Stack direction**: Stack grows downward (SP decreases on PUSH)
3. **String termination**: Strings must be null-terminated for OUTSTR
4. **Register encoding**: Registers are single bytes (0x00-0x07)
5. **Address sizes**: Jump addresses are 8-bit (0-255 program space)
6. **Division by zero**: Triggers runtime error
7. **Overflow detection**: Requires 32-bit overflow (use NOT + ADD for carry)

sections.title: Planned Opcodes (Not Yet Implemented)
sections.content:
**SIMD/SSE Operations (26 opcodes):** Vector operations for parallel processing

**AVX Operations (20 opcodes):** 256-bit vector operations

**MMX Operations (11 opcodes):** Multimedia/integer SIMD

**Extended 64-bit (18 opcodes):** Full 64-bit register operations

**Total:** 88 opcodes planned for future implementation

**Note:** Using unimplemented opcodes will result in an "Unknown opcode" error.
